label: fp16 baseline
response: Vision transformers (ViTs) are a type of deep learning model that process images by first converting them into a sequence of patches. Each patch is then represented as a vector of features, which are then fed into a series of self-attention layers. The self-attention layers allow the model to capture spatial relationships between different parts of the image by attending to the features of neighboring patches. This allows the model to learn more complex relationships between different parts of the image, which can improve its performance on tasks such as image classification and object detection.
